events.reflexive_note: Recording the demo turned the build into a communicable research object and exposed where my explanation still lagged behind the system.
The completion plan turned the remaining PhD work into a visible sequence of writing, validation and submission milestones.

After months of negotiation, the V&A formally endorsed the project as innovative and useful, while clarifying that access to uncatalogued material would depend on a light-touch data agreement around copyright, data usage, and practical handling. The message felt like a major institutional green light: it strengthened the direction of the research and made clear that access, governance, and collaboration were part of the method rather than just administrative hurdles.
events.evidence_link: Email from Matti Watton 13 Apr 2026; V&A Research Review Committee feedback
events.tags: v&a; archive; access; approval; data agreement; uncatalogued material

Mapping the database schema showed how ingestion, authority control and analytics had become part of the project's research infrastructure.
The three-strand framework clarified how historical inquiry, archival systems and computational methods could be held together as one research design.

links.source: conversation:V&A Research Review Committee approval email

events.reflexive_note: After months of negotiation, the V&A formally endorsed the project as innovative and useful, while clarifying that access to uncatalogued material would depend on a light-touch data agreement around copyright, data usage, and practical handling. The message felt like a major institutional green light: it strengthened the direction of the research and made clear that access, governance, and collaboration were part of the method rather than just administrative hurdles.
events.evidence_link: Email from Matti Watton 13 Apr 2026; V&A Research Review Committee feedback

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The reading helped justify a dataset made of coded practice events rather than only conventional metrics.

After months of negotiation, the V&A formally endorsed the project as innovative and useful, while clarifying that access to uncatalogued material would depend on a light-touch data agreement around copyright, data usage, and practical handling. The message felt like a major institutional green light: it strengthened the direction of the research and made clear that access, governance, and collaboration were part of the method rather than just administrative hurdles.
